interactions.body:
[RE:Affordability Confirmation] Hi Connie, 

Thanks for reaching out, we understand that it can seem difficult to find a cosigner at first. Don't worry though, we help people like you every day!

We're a cosigner lender, all of our customers must have a cosigner for us to lend to them. A cosigner is a great sign that someone trusts you, and that we should too regardless of your credit history! If your cosigner trusts you, then we will.

A cosigner can be ANYONE you know (family, friend, neighbor or even a colleague) who is a US resident, has a 700+ credit score and good history of paying their bills on time - someone who owns their home or pays a mortgage is much more likely to be accepted. Remember, as long as you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to make a payment! You might find some useful tips on how to approach finding or speaking to a potential cosigner here: https://transformcredit.com/cosigner-loans

When you have found someone, give them this link https://tcrdt.com/s/HAAEOGR0 - we'd love for your loan to add to the $100 million we've already paid out to people just like you. If you still find that you're not able to find a cosigner, please respond to this email with CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else.

Thanks, 
Jade@Transform Credit

interactions.body: Hi Vincent, We just need to confirm that you can afford the repayments. To continue, just use this link: https://tcrdt.com/s/PB1H7GWC Please make sure you are entering your income and expenses with no punctuation and selecting all the options in the drop-down menus. Unfortunately, the form fields are unable to read special characters such as commas or periods, and this is the most common reason why people are unable to proceed at this point. There is also a box you will need to check that says "Budget Plan Declaration." Once you have checked the box, you should see a large orange "Next" button appear at the bottom of the screen once the form is correctly filled out. If you have any questions, just let us know. Thanks, Together Loans

[Your Together Loans Loan Application : 0603053007152703130926] Hi Vincent,

Thank you for contacting Together Loans, your message is really important to us.

I can see that your application is active and you still have some tasks to finish before the loan is paid. Your current application contemplates a loan of $3000, over 24 months, paying back $167.73 monthly, but you can change these terms anytime before the loan is paid.

Together Loans is a cosigner-based lender.  This means all of our customers must have a cosigner for us to lend to them, regardless of the loan that you're applying for and your credit score. Normally, a cosigner is a friend or a family member who knows you (the borrower) and trusts that it is a good idea for you to borrow money. A suitable cosigner is someone who has a credit score of more than 700 and a strong history of paying their bills on time. Homeowners are more likely to be accepted even if they have a slightly lower score, as long as they have a history of paying their debts on time.

How does the application journey work? When you apply online, we will send you a unique link for your cosigner to register. As soon as we have everything we need from both of you, we’ll transfer the money. The money is transferred into your cosigner's account. This is to protect them, and us, against identity fraud, as transferring the money into their account ensures that they are fully aware of the loan.

You can check here more information about the application journey and common questions: https://togetherloans.com/faq 

To complete your application journey and check what is the next step, you can use this link: https://tcrdt.com/s/PB1H7GWC

Any other questions, please let me know.

Have a nice day,
Ceera@Together Loans

Hi Gavin, 
Take as long as you need, I will keep your application open for you, and when you are ready to continue, you can use this link: https://rgct.io/s/XT5UF30L
If you still don't have a cosigner, remember that a suitable cosigner is someone who has a credit score of more than 750 and a good history of paying their bills on time - homeowners are more likely to be accepted even if they have a slightly lower score, as long as they have a history of paying their debts on time. Most of our customers ask a close friend or family member to be their cosigner because they know and trust you the most.
If you have more questions, please let us know. 
Thanks, Lyric@TransformCredit

[Your TransformCredit Loan Application : 0619082633030807071631] Hi Kim,
 
Thanks for getting in touch.

When you applied for a loan with us, we offered two different products: Loans up to $7000 with a co-signer, and CreditBuilder which is a small loan held on our customer's behalf that customers make monthly payments against, building up their credit score.

I can see that you signed up for a Transform CreditBuilder account when applying for a loan of $5000, on <DateCreatedCb>. We sent you a ‘Welcome to Credit Builder’ email on sign-up, confirming your account, how it works and how you can manage it.

Your CreditBuilder helps build your credit, even if you do not borrow from us. When you sign up for CreditBuilder, you take the first step in building your score over 24 months. Each month you pay $5, which we report to the Credit Bureaus as interest against a loan that is held on your behalf. These successful payments build up your credit history, helping to improve your score. Once the 24 months have passed, or if you decide to cancel/stop paying, CreditBuilder is simply marked as paid in full. This prevents any missed payments from undoing the positive impact that your on-time payments have on your credit score.

You can find out more about CreditBuilder here: transformcredit.com/transform-credit-builder/ or manage your account yourself here, by logging in with your SSN and email: www.transformcredit.com/CBServicing/login. If you wish to cancel at any point, just go to settings to do so and we'll mark the loan as settled on your behalf.

CreditBuilder is a fantastic stepping stone towards seeing the benefits of an improving credit score, without the responsibility of taking on a big financial commitment. When you do find yourself ready, with the help of a co-signer, Transform Credit can still help you access funds of up to $7000 too! At which point, the CreditBuilder loan you have will be paid in full on your behalf.

If you need help, please let me know.
 
Many thanks, 
Michelle@TransformCredit

interactions.body:
[RE:Don't contact me again] Hi , 

Thanks for contacting Appeals. 

We understand that it may be disheartening or concerning if you've been considered an unsuitable cosigner, don't worry though - the most important thing is that we can still help the borrower.

There are a few reasons we could find someone to be unsuitable - the most common being a less than perfect credit history. Being a cosigner is a big financial commitment, they are responsible for making any loan payments that their borrower does not. Reviewing all cosigners' Experian credit histories allows us to check that they would be able to manage any payments they are required to make, in addition to maintaining any other existing commitments. If a cosigner has had missed or late payments in the past, we can't be certain that being a cosigner s a good idea and wouldn't see them struggle to keep their other accounts up to date. Alternatively, If a cosigner's credit file is locked when they apply, we won't have been able to confirm either way (if this has happened with you, you can reapply as soon as your credit file is unlocked: https://tcrdt.com/s/K0WX9PLD). 

A cosigner must have a 750+ credit score and good history of paying their bills on time (homeowners and mortgage payers are more likely to be accepted). It's important to remember that a cosigner can only be on one loan with Together Loans at a time too. If you'd like to review the information that we used in our assessment of your credit, you can check your Experian credit report/score for free at: https://www.experian.com/. 

In the meantime, the $0 will be reserved for the borrower. We'd love for their loan to add to the $100 million we've already paid out to people just like them. If you have any other questions, you may find the answer here: https://togetherloans.com/faq.

Jade@Together Loans

Hi Debra, thanks for your call. None of our agents are available, please reply here with your question and we'll get back to you. Thanks, Together Loans

Hi Debra, we can't payout your loan until you have a suitable cosigner who has completed their application. Our lending decision is based on trust, providing a cosigner who trusts you enables us to lend to you even if you've been turned down elsewhere. A suitable cosigner is someone who has a credit score of more than 750 and a good history of paying their bills on time (homeowners are more likely to be accepted). If you haven't already added a cosigner, send them this link to register online in 2 minutes: https://tcrdt.com/s/JD1YU21N If you have any more questions, please let us know. Thanks, Together Loans

interactions.label: Application closed — 7 tasks finalised in bulk

interactions.body:
Hi Debra,
Thank you for reaching out, and we apologize for the delayed response. I just want to reassure you that Together Loans is a real and fully licensed lender. We’ve been helping customers for over seven years and are based in Chicago, incorporated in Delaware. We currently lend across 23 different states, all of which require us to be licensed and to follow strict regulations.We are registered with the Nationwide Multistate Licensing System (NMLS) under licence number 1905055 and undergo annual audits to ensure we remain in full compliance. Our NMLS registration may still appear under our former name, Transform Credit, as we recently rebranded — but our licensing, ownership, and regulatory standing remain unchanged.For extra peace of mind, you can look us up anytime on the official NMLS website: www.nmlsconsumeraccess.org.
We also sent you an email explaining the reason your application was declined, so please have a look when you can.
If you need any help or have any questions, I’m right here.
Thanks,Together Loans

interactions.body: Hi Tereina, all of our customers must have a cosigner. It's a great sign someone trusts you, so we should too! They can be ANYONE who has a 700+ credit and good history of paying their bills. Homeowners with slightly lower scores will also have a good chance of approval. Remember, if you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to pay! Find tips here: https://togetherloans.com/cosigner-loans. When you do find someone, give them this link https://tglns.com/s/921R5JMI - we'd love to add you to the 40,000 customers we have already helped like you. If you still can't find a cosigner, please reply CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else. Ben, Together Loans

Thank you for reaching out.

Upon review, I can confirm we have not received either of your new cosigner's applications. Please, use this link to invite them: https://tglns.com/s/921R5JMI

If you need any help, just let me know. 

Thanks,

[Your TransformCredit Loan Application : 0607062225073002002700] Hi Tyron,

A cosigner is someone who trusts you, knows you well and agrees to make the repayments if you don’t. We're able to lend to you based on this trust. 

A suitable cosigner is someone who has a credit score of more than 750 and good history of paying their bills on time (homeowners are more likely to be accepted). Most of our borrowers ask a close friend or family member to be their cosigner.

Just send this link to your cosigner and they can register online in 2 minutes: https://rgct.io/s/0IUAGESI and if you have any more questions, please let us know.

Thanks, 
Niall@TransformCredit

Transform Credit requires a cosigner, regardless of the loan that you're applying for and your credit score. A cosigner shows us that someone trusts you to repay your loan and that we should too! This trust allows us to lend to you even if you're been turned down elsewhere. 

A suitable cosigner is someone who has a credit score of more than 750 and good history of paying their bills on time (homeowners are more likely to be accepted). Most of our borrowers ask a close friend or family member to be their cosigner.

Hi Marvin, 
Thank you for contacting Transform Credit. Unfortunately we cannot continue with Paulina as your co-signer.
At this time, our infrastructure only supports being able to lend to those that can understand English - our contracts, website, communications, and staff are not set up to support other languages, although this is something we are excited to develop in the future so that we can help more people that do not speak English. 
Being a borrower or a cosigner is a serious responsibility and it would be unlawful for us to lend to someone where we knew there was an obvious language barrier, putting that person into a vulnerable situation where they may not fully understand the agreement they are entering into. 
We appreciate your understanding.

You can use this link to invite someone new to co-sign https://tcrdt.com/s/QZEJ26FR
Thanks, Transform Credit

[RE:Affordability Confirmation] Hi Sara Maria, 

Thanks for reaching out, we understand that it can seem difficult to find a cosigner at first. Don't worry though, we help people like you every day!

We're a cosigner lender, all of our customers must have a cosigner for us to lend to them. A cosigner is a great sign that someone trusts you, and that we should too regardless of your credit history! If your cosigner trusts you, then we will.

A cosigner can be ANYONE you know (family, friend, neighbor or even a colleague) who is a US resident, has a 700+ credit score and good history of paying their bills on time - someone who owns their home or pays a mortgage is much more likely to be accepted. Remember, as long as you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to make a payment! You might find some useful tips on how to approach finding or speaking to a potential cosigner here: https://transformcredit.com/cosigner-loans

When you have found someone, give them this link https://tcrdt.com/s/1X3KHERN - we'd love for your loan to add to the $100 million we've already paid out to people just like you. If you still find that you're not able to find a cosigner, please respond to this email with CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else.

Thanks, 
Jade@Transform Credit

[RE:CreditBuilder Payment Reminder] Hi Sara Maris,
 
Thanks for getting in touch.
 
I can see that you signed up for a CreditBuilder account AND applied for a $3000 loan on 02/03/2026. 

When you sign up for CreditBuilder, you take the first step in building your score over 1-2 years. Each month you pay $5, which we report to the Credit Bureaus as payment for a loan that is held on your behalf. These successful payments build up your credit history, helping to improve your score. Once the loan term is completed, or if you decide to cancel/stop paying, your CreditBuilder account is simply marked as paid in full. This prevents any missed payments from undoing the positive impact that your on-time payments have on your credit score.

CreditBuilder is a fantastic stepping stone towards seeing the benefits of an improving credit score, without the responsibility of taking on a big financial commitment. When you do find yourself ready, with the help of a co-signer, Together Loans can help you access funds too! At which point, any CreditBuilder loan you have will be paid in full on your behalf.

You can find out more about the Together Loans CreditBuilder here - https://togetherloans.com/together-loans-credit-builder/ . However, I can confirm that I have canceled your CreditBuilder account, you will not be charged again. 

We still have your loan application approved for you. All you need is a cosigner, a friend or a family member who has a 750+ credit score and agrees to make the payment if you can't. Follow this link to pick up where you left off: https://tcrdt.com/s/OMT0ZQYX

If you need help, please let me know
 
Have a great day, 
Jade @ Together Loans CreditBuilder

Hi Anthony, We received your message about cancelling the loan application. I appreciate our loans may seem expensive, but in context, our interest rate is not high at all. Lending to customers who have bad credit or no credit at all is more risky and typically if you have a damaged credit score or no credit at all, there is no other option than pay-day lenders whose interest rate can be in the hundreds of percent. 
 
We offer a more flexible loan option and we don’t believe in hidden fees or charges. Additionally, a lot of our customers pay off the loans early when their financial situation improves, which saves them a lot of money in interest because our interest is calculated daily so you only pay for the time you borrow. You can settle early to save on interest, without the penalties that other companies charge, if you chose to do this.   
Thanks, Together Loans

Hi rose,
Thank you for reaching out. We understand your concerns and I want to reassure you that we are a legitimate lender, incorporated in Delaware and based primarily out of Chicago. We have been lending for over 7 years, across 22 different states, all of which require licensing. You can find this information on our website: https://togetherloans.com/Licenses. We are also registered with Nationwide Multistate Licensing System (NMLS), with license number 1905055. We have annual state audits in order to maintain our licenses, and if there were any significant issues, we would be unable to lend. We are currently applying for accreditation with the BBB which means we will be able to point customers to review our services there also, giving us a real rating. BBB has also investigated our business and was satisfied with our responses, which have been published on our page. I hope this information provides you with the peace of mind needed to move forward with us. Please let us know if you have any further questions or concerns.
Thanks,
Together Loans

[Your Together Loans Loan Application : 0609083329021905210723] Hi rose,

We pay the funds out to you rather than the borrower for 3 key reasons. These are:

1) To verify your identity and prevent identity fraud from being committed against you, as you have to provide us with an account that is active, valid, in your name and capable of ACH payments that we pay the funds into. 
2) To make sure that you are fully aware of the loan and provide you with an opportunity to return to the funds to us, if you are unsure on whether this financial responsibility is something you want to continue with.
3) By transferring the funds to you to transfer to the borrower, you're confirming your roles and responsibilities on this loan. Additionally, because you agree to make any payments that the borrower does not, we can then use these bank details as a backup payment method if the account goes past due. 

Being a cosigner is a serious financial commitment and it is important that any cosigner fully understands their role and the responsibilities, and obligations that come with becoming a co-signer. We believe that paying the funds out to the co-signer allows them to confirm their understanding and make a fully informed decision, before committing to these responsibilities.  

If you have any other questions, please let me know. 

Thanks, 
Divya@Together Loans
